findings.summary: MCP kb_add accepted an empty/whitespace path, writing 'text → ' — the instance-5 pointer-to-nowhere reintroduced via the degenerate value (fixed in 3b34510; revert-tested red)
--tag '' / --tag ,,, silently meant 'no filter' on list and 'untagged' on add — flagList returned [] (fixed in 3b34510; revert-tested red)
list --status was not validated while --type was — a typo printed nothing with exit 0 (fixed in 3b34510; revert-tested red)
no '--' end-of-options escape: entry text whose first token starts with '--' cannot be added — loud rejection accepted as better than the old silent mangling

empty --tag on add renders via add's local try/catch, so the error prints without the usage block the top-level UsageError handler appends — cosmetic inconsistency, correct exit code and no brain write
search --status and add --status/--role/--zone/--prov-status VALUES remain unvalidated ('as any' casts) — pre-existing, outside issue #95 scope; search mitigated by the RFC-002 NO-MATCH diagnosis
MCP kb_add tags:'' still lands an untagged entry silently, and type=link with empty text yields a label-less ' → x' body — the empty-value class survives on the zod surface; follow-up material
